action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 19 eval failed with 'Missing key(s) in state_dict: inc.residual.weight, ...' when loading iter_14/best_model.pt. This indicates the current model.py expects residual submodules (inc.residual, up1.conv.residual) that the checkpoint does not have. The checkpoints from earlier successful evals (iter 18) loaded fine, so the model.py must have been changed after iter 18 (likely accidentally). The residual keys are not present in the checkpoints, meaning the trained models never had them. We need to revert model.py to the architecture that matches the checkpoints (no residual blocks). Time remaining 0.31h — urgent. Decision: Rewrite model.py with a standard UNet (DoubleConv blocks, no residual inside inc or up convs). Verify loading of iter_14 checkpoint with a quick test command, then resubmit eval with the ensemble predict.py. If loading succeeds, we can quickly resubmit to secure the ensemble score.
action.command: python -c "import torch; from model import WeatherUNet; ckpt = torch.load('logs/iter_14/best_model.pt', map_location='cpu'); sd = ckpt['model_state_dict']; shape = sd['inc.conv1.weight'].shape; print('in_channels:', shape[1], 'base_channels:', shape[0]); model = WeatherUNet(in_channels=shape[1], out_channels=2, base_channels=shape[0], dropout=0.15); model.load_state_dict(sd); print('Success: iter_14 checkpoint loaded'); ckpt8 = torch.load('logs/iter_8/best_model.pt', map_location='cpu'); sd8 = ckpt8['model_state_dict']; model8 = WeatherUNet(in_channels=sd8['inc.conv1.weight'].shape[1], out_channels=2, base_channels=sd8['inc.conv1.weight'].shape[0], dropout=0.0); model8.load_state_dict(sd8); print('Success: iter_8 checkpoint loaded'); print('All checkpoints compatible')"
